deck: Three of Dimagi's mental health experts join Jonathan Jackson to unpack the global mental health gap, the power of frontline and self-help group models, and where digital tools can scale care without losing the human connection that makes it work.
seo_description: Dimagi experts on scaling mental health care in lower and middle income countries through frontline workers, self-help groups, and digital tools that keep humans central.
og_description: Scaling mental health care through frontline workers, self-help groups, and digital tools that keep human connection central.

pull_quote.text: When you're looking at trying to treat tuberculosis, you can't just treat the disease, you have to treat the person as well.
pull_quote.cite: Christie Civetta, on integrating mental and physical health

bullets: A stark inequity: why depression is projected to be the third leading cause of disease burden in lower and middle income countries by 2030, with most people receiving no treatment at all
The view from Ghana: Anaba Sunday Atua on stigma, cultural barriers, and the limited resources that keep mental health relegated to the background
Self-help groups that work: peer led community models that build social support, fight stigma, and connect people back to formal care
Frontline workers at the center: training community health and lay workers to deliver brief, evidence-based psychological interventions
Mental and physical health intertwined: how integrating mental health into TB, HIV, and maternal care improves adherence and outcomes
Digital with a human touch: where self-guided apps, chatbots, and AI can help, and why personal connection still cannot be replaced
Unlocking scale: using digital tools to ease the training and supervision bottlenecks that have held quality interventions back

stats.label: of people in lower and middle income countries who do not receive any treatment for depression
of government funding committed to mental health each year in Ghana, per a WHO report
TB infections that could be reduced by 2030 if mental health care were integrated into TB care

guests.name: Lauren Magoun
guests.role: Senior Health Strategy Specialist on the research and data team, advancing mental health programs through digital tools.

guests.name: Anaba Sunday Atua
guests.role: Senior Project Analyst with a public health background and nearly a decade supporting mental health at the frontlines in Ghana.

guests.name: Christie Civetta
guests.role: Senior mental health strategy consultant with a background in crisis counseling and tech for development, supporting Dimagi's mental health offerings.
